rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (reduce_scatter verification gap in test_expert_parallel_layout.py), so recall is low for both, but Y at least raises an analogous test-coverage gap (missing branch coverage in the DP-metadata tests) while X explicitly asserts test coverage is adequate, moving further from the reviewer's spirit. Y's findings are more actionable — each has a precise file:line, cites cross-referenced vLLM source, and proposes a concrete fix (docstring, comment, assertion, test case) — whereas X mostly emits blanket PASS verdicts and offers only one soft nitpick before recommending APPROVE. Both are well-grounded with no fabrications, but Y's itemized, evidence-backed format is more useful to a reviewer.
